title: Build a Profile Card
description: Create a simple profile card using semantic HTML. Add a profile image, a name heading, a short bio, and a button. Structure it correctly using the right HTML elements.
inputFormat: No user input required — just build the correct HTML structure.
outputFormat: A visible profile card with an image, name (h2), bio paragraph, and a button with id 'contact-btn'.
constraints: Use a <section> element with id 'profile-card' as the outer container.
The name must be inside an <h2> tag with id 'profile-name'.

The bio must be inside a

tag with id 'profile-bio'.
Include an tag with id 'profile-img' inside the card.
Include a <button> with id 'contact-btn' and text 'Contact Me'.

examples.output: A profile card showing an image, name, bio paragraph, and a 'Contact Me' button.

initialJs:
// No JavaScript needed for this challenge.
// Just focus on building the correct HTML structure!
changesToBeDone: Replace the <div id='profile-card'> with a <section id='profile-card'>.
Add an with a valid src attribute inside the card.
Add an <h2 id='profile-name'> with any name text.

Add a

with a short bio sentence.
Add a <button id='contact-btn'> with text 'Contact Me'.
hints: Change the outer `<div id='profile-card'>` to `<section id='profile-card'>`.
Add `` inside the section.

Add `

Jane Doe

` below the image.

Add `

A passionate web developer.

` below the name.
Add `<button id='contact-btn'>Contact Me</button>` at the bottom.

rules.errorMessage: Missing <section> element with id 'profile-card'. Replace the <div> with <section>.
The outer container must be a <section>, not a <div>.
Missing inside the profile card.
Missing <h2 id='profile-name'> for the profile name.

Missing

for the profile bio.
The button text must be 'Contact Me'.

solutionHtml:
Jane Doe

A passionate web developer who loves building beautiful UIs.
solutionJs: // No JavaScript needed for this challenge.

title: Style a Flexbox Navbar
description: Style a navigation bar using CSS Flexbox. The logo should be on the left and the navigation links should be on the right. Make the navbar look polished with a dark background and hover effects on the links.
inputFormat: No user input — just write CSS to style the existing HTML structure.
outputFormat: A horizontal navbar with the logo left-aligned and nav links right-aligned.
Nav links change color on hover.
constraints: The <nav id='main-nav'> must use display: flex.
The logo and links must be on opposite ends (space-between).
Nav links must have a hover color change.
The navbar background must not be white.

examples.output: The nav link changes its color on hover.

initialHtml:
MyBrand
  • Home
  • About
  • Contact
initialJs: // No JavaScript needed for this challenge.
changesToBeDone: Set display: flex on #main-nav.
Set justify-content: space-between on #main-nav so logo is left and links are right.
Set align-items: center on #main-nav.
Add a dark background color to #main-nav (not white).
Add padding to #main-nav.
Set display: flex and gap on #nav-links.
Add a hover color (not #cbd5e1) on #nav-links a:hover.
Add a transition on #nav-links a for smooth hover effect.
hints: Add `display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;` to `#main-nav`.

rules.errorMessage: The navbar background must not be white.
#nav-links must have display: flex to align links horizontally.
You must add a :hover style for #nav-links a.

solutionHtml:
MyBrand
  • Home
  • About
  • Contact
solutionJs: // No JavaScript needed for this challenge.

title: Interactive Counter App
description: Build an interactive counter using JavaScript. There are three buttons — Increment, Decrement, and Reset. The counter value must be shown in a <span> element. The count must never go below 0, and must change color when it reaches 10 or above.
inputFormat: A button with id 'btn-increment' to increase the counter.
A button with id 'btn-decrement' to decrease the counter (minimum 0).
A button with id 'btn-reset' to reset counter to 0.
outputFormat: A <span id='count-display'> showing the current count.
Count changes to red color when >= 10.
Count stays at 0 when decrement is clicked at 0.
constraints: Use let or const only (no var).
The count must not go below 0.
When count >= 10, the #count-display color must be red.
When count < 10, the color must revert to the default (not red).
The console must have no errors.

examples.output: Count resets to 0 and color reverts.

initialHtml:
Counter

0
changesToBeDone: Declare 'count' using let (not var), starting at 0.
Get references to #count-display, #btn-increment, #btn-decrement, #btn-reset.
Write updateDisplay() to update the span text and change color to red when count >= 10.
On increment button click, increase count by 1 and call updateDisplay().
On decrement button click, decrease count only if > 0, then call updateDisplay().
On reset button click, set count to 0 and call updateDisplay().
Ensure the console has no errors.
hints: Use `document.getElementById('count-display')` to get the display element.
In updateDisplay(): set `display.textContent = count;` and check `if (count >= 10) { display.style.color = 'red'; } else { display.style.color = ''; }`

rules.errorMessage: Do not use 'var'. Use 'let' or 'const' instead.
Fix any JavaScript errors shown in the console.
You must declare a 'count' variable in your JavaScript.
Clicking Increment should increase count to 1 and show it in #count-display.
Clicking Reset should bring count back to 0 in #count-display.

solutionHtml:
Counter

0
